SQ_A3_Bbeta Counting / Cumulative × Variable Content Response ✓
A3 Counting / Cumulative — Not every event is reported — the model reports only when a cumulative state is reached.
B-β Variable Content Response — The response depends on visual details of the current frame — tests event detection plus attribute recognition.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic systematically removing all the lug nuts from a car's four wheels using an impact wrench. The chosen question fits the cell because it requires counting a repeated action (removing a lug nut) and only reporting on a cumulative total (every 5th nut). This fulfills the A3 counting trigger. Additionally, the required response must identify which specific wheel was just completed, which varies for each trigger, thus satisfying the B-beta variable content response requirement.

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    Q: After every 5th lug nut is removed, tell me which wheel was just completed.
    t (mm:ss)response_windowresponse_text
    00:50.7[00:50.7, 00:51.7]The lug nuts on the front passenger side wheel have been removed.
    01:52.5[01:52.5, 01:53.5]The lug nuts on the rear passenger side wheel have been removed.
    02:13.3[02:13.3, 02:14.3]The lug nuts on the rear driver's side wheel have been removed.
    02:30.4[02:30.4, 02:31.4]The lug nuts on the front driver's side wheel have been removed.
Show full per-cell prompt sent to Gemini 
You are designing proactive streaming-video benchmark samples — questions AND their ground-truth response timestamps — for a single video.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an SQ (Single Question, multiple answers) task, the model receives a single question at some point in time and must then continuously track it, triggering a response each time a matching event occurs in the video stream. Responses are not one-shot. A typical example: "Remind me when a balloon appears" — each time a balloon appears the model must produce a response.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: SQ_A3_Bbeta

Axis 1 — A3 Counting / Cumulative
Definition: Not every event is reported — the model reports only when a cumulative state is reached.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: Give me the total count every 10 stirs
  - Manipulation: Report after every 3 pick-and-place actions
  - Navigation: Tell me every time I pass 5 doors

Axis 2 — B-β Variable Content Response
Definition: The response depends on visual details of the current frame — tests event detection plus attribute recognition.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: Tell me which ingredient I'm picking up each time (carrot / onion / egg)
  - Manipulation: Tell me the color and shape of the object I'm grasping each time
  - Navigation: Tell me the color of the door and whether it's open each time

Reference combined example (Kitchen domain): Every 10 stirs, tell me what food is being stirred

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis 1 (Trigger Pattern) rule:
  Counting / cumulative — emit a trigger only every Nth chronological occurrence of the underlying action (N is part of the question, e.g. every 10 stirs). Skip the intermediate events.

Axis 2 (Response Complexity) rule:
  Variable content response — response_text varies per event with the visual attribute of THAT event (e.g. the specific ingredient name, the object's color and shape, whether the door is open). Different events should generally produce different response_text.

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.
  - Distractor events (events that LOOK like triggers but should NOT produce a response — e.g. red curtains for a 'red door' question, or doors after a cancel-doors command) MUST be omitted from gt_responses. Any model output at a non-GT timestamp counts as a false positive.
  - Every trigger_time must be grounded in an event you actually observe in this specific video — do not fabricate timestamps.

[TASK]
Produce exactly 1 SQ sample(s). Each sample is one question string plus the full list of gt_responses for that question.

Each sample must:
  (a) match this cell's trigger pattern AND response complexity,
  (b) be grounded in events / objects / states actually present in this specific video — no generic templates,
  (c) yield MULTIPLE gt_responses across the video's duration (at least 2 entries; prefer 3-8),
  (d) demonstrate this cell's combination distinctively.

Return STRICT JSON conforming to the schema. Do not include any commentary outside the JSON.

  "model": "gemini-2.5-pro",
  "prompt_version": "v1",
  "samples_per_cell": 1,
  "timing": {
    "upload_s": 0.0,
    "generate_s": 22.094,
    "total_s": 22.094
  },
  "generated_at": "2026-05-19T19:04:58",
  "video_uri": "https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/files/57a6np6urdmo"
}
SQ_A3_Bgamma Counting / Cumulative × Stateful Response ✓
A3 Counting / Cumulative — Not every event is reported — the model reports only when a cumulative state is reached.
B-γ Stateful Response — The response depends on accumulated history — tests event detection plus long-term memory or counting.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic removing all 20 lug nuts from the wheels of a Jeep. The question asks for an update every 4th time a lug nut is removed, stating the cumulative total at that point. This fits the A3 (Counting / Cumulative) trigger pattern because it only reports on the 4th, 8th, 12th, etc., events, and it fits B-gamma (Stateful Response) because the response text includes the total count, which requires remembering the previous events.

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    Q: Every 4th time a lug nut is removed from a wheel, tell me the total number of lug nuts that have been taken off so far.
    t (mm:ss)response_windowresponse_text
    00:49.0[00:49.0, 00:50.0]A total of 4 lug nuts have been removed.
    01:08.0[01:08.0, 01:09.0]A total of 8 lug nuts have been removed.
    01:27.0[01:27.0, 01:28.0]A total of 12 lug nuts have been removed.
    01:41.0[01:41.0, 01:42.0]A total of 16 lug nuts have been removed.
    01:50.0[01:50.0, 01:51.0]A total of 20 lug nuts have been removed.

You are designing proactive streaming-video benchmark samples — questions AND their ground-truth response timestamps — for a single video.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an SQ (Single Question, multiple answers) task, the model receives a single question at some point in time and must then continuously track it, triggering a response each time a matching event occurs in the video stream. Responses are not one-shot. A typical example: "Remind me when a balloon appears" — each time a balloon appears the model must produce a response.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: SQ_A3_Bgamma

Axis 1 — A3 Counting / Cumulative
Definition: Not every event is reported — the model reports only when a cumulative state is reached.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: Give me the total count every 10 stirs
  - Manipulation: Report after every 3 pick-and-place actions
  - Navigation: Tell me every time I pass 5 doors

Axis 2 — B-γ Stateful Response
Definition: The response depends on accumulated history — tests event detection plus long-term memory or counting.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: Each time I pick up the knife, tell me which time this is
  - Manipulation: Say 'new' when I grasp a new object; say which repeat number when I grasp a repeated one
  - Navigation: Each time I enter a new room, say its index in order of appearance

Reference combined example (Kitchen domain): Every 10 stirs, tell me the cumulative total

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis 1 (Trigger Pattern) rule:
  Counting / cumulative — emit a trigger only every Nth chronological occurrence of the underlying action (N is part of the question, e.g. every 10 stirs). Skip the intermediate events.

Axis 2 (Response Complexity) rule:
  Stateful response — response_text is computed from the accumulated history of prior events (e.g. a count, an ordinal like '3rd time', a 'new'/'repeat #2' marker). The text necessarily depends on all earlier entries within the same question.

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.
  - Distractor events (events that LOOK like triggers but should NOT produce a response — e.g. red curtains for a 'red door' question, or doors after a cancel-doors command) MUST be omitted from gt_responses. Any model output at a non-GT timestamp counts as a false positive.
  - Every trigger_time must be grounded in an event you actually observe in this specific video — do not fabricate timestamps.

SQ_A4_Balpha Conditional Threshold × Fixed Response ✓
A4 Conditional Threshold — The model must continuously monitor a state and respond only when a threshold condition is met.
B-α Fixed Response — The same content is said every time — isolates event-detection capability.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic removing all four wheels from a Jeep. The question asks the model to identify the moment when the final lug nut has been removed from each wheel. This fits the A4 conditional threshold cell because it requires monitoring the state of lug nut removal for each wheel and only responding when the process is complete, and it fits the B-alpha fixed response cell because the same response is required for each instance.

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    Q: Tell me each time all the lug nuts have been removed from a wheel by saying 'All nuts removed'.
    t (mm:ss)response_windowresponse_text
    00:51.5[00:51.5, 00:52.5]All nuts removed
    01:12.5[01:12.5, 01:13.5]All nuts removed
    01:33.5[01:33.5, 01:34.5]All nuts removed
    01:50.5[01:50.5, 01:51.5]All nuts removed

You are designing proactive streaming-video benchmark samples — questions AND their ground-truth response timestamps — for a single video.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an SQ (Single Question, multiple answers) task, the model receives a single question at some point in time and must then continuously track it, triggering a response each time a matching event occurs in the video stream. Responses are not one-shot. A typical example: "Remind me when a balloon appears" — each time a balloon appears the model must produce a response.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: SQ_A4_Balpha

Axis 1 — A4 Conditional Threshold
Definition: The model must continuously monitor a state and respond only when a threshold condition is met.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: Alert me when the water starts boiling
  - Manipulation: Warn me when an object is about to fall
  - Navigation: Alert me when I'm within 1 meter of an obstacle

Axis 2 — B-α Fixed Response
Definition: The same content is said every time — isolates event-detection capability.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: Say 'cutting' every time I start cutting
  - Manipulation: Say 'grasp' every time I grasp something
  - Navigation: Say 'door' every time you see a door

Reference combined example (Kitchen domain): Say 'boiling' when the water boils

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis 1 (Trigger Pattern) rule:
  Conditional threshold — emit a trigger at the frame where the threshold / state-change condition first becomes true. If the condition becomes true multiple disjoint times, emit one trigger per onset.

Axis 2 (Response Complexity) rule:
  Fixed response — use the SAME canonical response_text for every entry, taken from the question's template (e.g. 'cutting', 'door', 'grasp').

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.
  - Distractor events (events that LOOK like triggers but should NOT produce a response — e.g. red curtains for a 'red door' question, or doors after a cancel-doors command) MUST be omitted from gt_responses. Any model output at a non-GT timestamp counts as a false positive.
  - Every trigger_time must be grounded in an event you actually observe in this specific video — do not fabricate timestamps.

Produce exactly 1 SQ sample(s). Each sample is one question string plus the full list of gt_responses for that question.

Each sample must:
  (a) match this cell's trigger pattern AND response complexity,
  (b) be grounded in events / objects / states actually present in this specific video — no generic templates,
  (c) yield MULTIPLE gt_responses across the video's duration (at least 2 entries; prefer 3-8),
  (d) demonstrate this cell's combination distinctively.

SQ_A4_Bbeta Conditional Threshold × Variable Content Response ✓
A4 Conditional Threshold — The model must continuously monitor a state and respond only when a threshold condition is met.
B-β Variable Content Response — The response depends on visual details of the current frame — tests event detection plus attribute recognition.
Rationale

The video shows a mechanic removing all four wheels from a car. The chosen question asks the model to identify the moment all lug nuts are removed from each wheel, which represents a clear conditional threshold (the wheel is fully unbolted). The response requires specifying the location of the wheel (e.g., front right, rear left), making the response content variable based on the visual context of each event. This perfectly matches the A4 (Conditional Threshold) and B-beta (Variable Content Response) criteria.

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    Q: Let me know each time all the lug nuts are removed from a wheel, and tell me which wheel it was.
    t (mm:ss)response_windowresponse_text
    00:51.3[00:51.3, 00:52.3]All lug nuts have been removed from the front right wheel.
    01:12.4[01:12.4, 01:13.4]All lug nuts have been removed from the rear right wheel.
    01:33.4[01:33.4, 01:34.4]All lug nuts have been removed from the rear left wheel.
    01:50.6[01:50.6, 01:51.6]All lug nuts have been removed from the front left wheel.

In an SQ (Single Question, multiple answers) task, the model receives a single question at some point in time and must then continuously track it, triggering a response each time a matching event occurs in the video stream. Responses are not one-shot. A typical example: "Remind me when a balloon appears" — each time a balloon appears the model must produce a response.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: SQ_A4_Bbeta

Axis 1 — A4 Conditional Threshold
Definition: The model must continuously monitor a state and respond only when a threshold condition is met.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: Alert me when the water starts boiling
  - Manipulation: Warn me when an object is about to fall
  - Navigation: Alert me when I'm within 1 meter of an obstacle

Axis 2 — B-β Variable Content Response
Definition: The response depends on visual details of the current frame — tests event detection plus attribute recognition.
Examples across domains:

  - Manipulation: Tell me the color and shape of the object I'm grasping each time

Axis 2 (Response Complexity) rule:
  Variable content response — response_text varies per event with the visual attribute of THAT event (e.g. the specific ingredient name, the object's color and shape, whether the door is open). Different events should generally produce different response_text.

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.

SQ_A4_Bgamma Conditional Threshold × Stateful Response ✓
A4 Conditional Threshold — The model must continuously monitor a state and respond only when a threshold condition is met.
B-γ Stateful Response — The response depends on accumulated history — tests event detection plus long-term memory or counting.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic in a garage removing all four wheels from a vehicle on a lift. The selected question asks the model to respond each time all the lug nuts from one wheel have been removed, and to count which wheel this is. This task fits the A4 conditional threshold, as it requires monitoring the state of each wheel and only triggering when the count of remaining lug nuts reaches zero. It also exemplifies the B-gamma stateful response by requiring a cumulative count of the completed wheels in the response.

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    Q: Let me know each time all of the lug nuts have been taken off a wheel, and tell me the count.
    t (mm:ss)response_windowresponse_text
    00:51.5[00:51.5, 00:52.5]This is the 1st wheel to have all its lug nuts removed.
    01:52.5[01:52.5, 01:53.5]This is the 2nd wheel to have all its lug nuts removed.
    02:13.5[02:13.5, 02:14.5]This is the 3rd wheel to have all its lug nuts removed.
    02:30.5[02:30.5, 02:31.5]This is the 4th wheel to have all its lug nuts removed.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an SQ (Single Question, multiple answers) task, the model receives a single question at some point in time and must then continuously track it, triggering a response each time a matching event occurs in the video stream. Responses are not one-shot. A typical example: "Remind me when a balloon appears" — each time a balloon appears the model must produce a response.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: SQ_A4_Bgamma

Axis 1 — A4 Conditional Threshold
Definition: The model must continuously monitor a state and respond only when a threshold condition is met.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: Alert me when the water starts boiling
  - Manipulation: Warn me when an object is about to fall
  - Navigation: Alert me when I'm within 1 meter of an obstacle

Axis 2 — B-γ Stateful Response
Definition: The response depends on accumulated history — tests event detection plus long-term memory or counting.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: Each time I pick up the knife, tell me which time this is
  - Manipulation: Say 'new' when I grasp a new object; say which repeat number when I grasp a repeated one
  - Navigation: Each time I enter a new room, say its index in order of appearance

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis 1 (Trigger Pattern) rule:
  Conditional threshold — emit a trigger at the frame where the threshold / state-change condition first becomes true. If the condition becomes true multiple disjoint times, emit one trigger per onset.

Axis 2 (Response Complexity) rule:
  Stateful response — response_text is computed from the accumulated history of prior events (e.g. a count, an ordinal like '3rd time', a 'new'/'repeat #2' marker). The text necessarily depends on all earlier entries within the same question.

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.

MQ_C1_D1 Independent × Sequential / Non-overlapping ✓
C1 Independent — Multiple questions coexist but are semantically unrelated; the model must track them in parallel without interference.
D1 Sequential / Non-overlapping — A new question is issued only after the previous one has completed or been explicitly closed. Active scopes do not overlap.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic working on a vehicle's wheels and brakes. The sample illustrates the MQ_C1_D1 cell by posing two independent, sequential questions. The first question tracks each time the mechanic places lug nuts in a tray, an action that occurs multiple times. Once this phase of the work is complete, the first question is deactivated and a new, semantically unrelated question about removing the wheels is activated. This non-overlapping, sequential structure for independent tasks perfectly embodies the C1 (Independent) and D1 (Sequential) axes.

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    qidissuedactivedeactive depends_onquestion
    q_00100:05.000:05.001:57.0—Notify me each time the mechanic places a set of lug nuts into the magnetic tray.
    q_00201:57.001:57.0null—Now, alert me each time a wheel is removed from the car.
    qidt (mm:ss)response_window response_text
    q_00100:55.6[00:55.6, 00:56.6]The mechanic has placed lug nuts in the magnetic tray.
    q_00101:15.2[01:15.2, 01:16.2]The mechanic has placed another set of lug nuts in the magnetic tray.
    q_00101:35.3[01:35.3, 01:36.3]The mechanic has placed another set of lug nuts in the magnetic tray.
    q_00101:55.7[01:55.7, 01:56.7]The mechanic has placed the final set of lug nuts in the magnetic tray.
    q_00201:59.8[01:59.8, 02:00.8]The mechanic has removed a wheel from the car.
    q_00202:15.2[02:15.2, 02:16.2]The mechanic has removed another wheel from the car.
Show full per-cell prompt sent to Gemini 
You are designing proactive streaming-video benchmark samples — questions AND their ground-truth response timestamps — for a single video.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an MQ (Multiple Questions, multiple answers) task, the model receives multiple questions at different points in time. Each question may require multiple responses (as in SQ), but the questions are interrelated — through logical dependencies, temporal relationships, or both. The model must concurrently track all active questions, manage their lifecycles, and respond to the appropriate question at the appropriate moment.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: MQ_C1_D1

Axis 1 — C1 Independent
Definition: Multiple questions coexist but are semantically unrelated; the model must track them in parallel without interference.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: "Remind me when I pick up the knife" + "Count how many times I add seasoning"
  - Manipulation: "Alert me on each grasp" + "Count completed assembly steps"
  - Navigation: "Alert me when I see a door" + "Count my turns"

Axis 2 — D1 Sequential / Non-overlapping
Definition: A new question is issued only after the previous one has completed or been explicitly closed. Active scopes do not overlap.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: t=0 "Alert me when I finish cutting" → (after cutting) → t=60 "Now alert me when I add water"
  - Manipulation: t=0 "Alert me when I finish tightening" → (done) → t=45 "Next, alert me when I pick up the next tool"
  - Navigation: t=0 "Tell me when I reach the kitchen" → (arrived) → t=30 "Now tell me when I reach the bedroom"

Reference combined example: see the cross-product of the two axes above — your task is to instantiate that combination in this video.

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis C (Logical Relationship between questions) rule:
  Independent — questions are semantically unrelated. GT for each question is generated independently and merged. `depends_on` stays empty for every question.

Axis D (Temporal Relationship between questions) rule:
  Sequential / non-overlapping — questions do not overlap in time. Each question's GT is restricted to events within [issue_time, next_question_issue_time). Set deactivation_time of each question to the next question's issue_time (or null for the last question).

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.
  - Distractor events (events that LOOK like triggers but should NOT produce a response — e.g. red curtains for a 'red door' question, or doors after a cancel-doors command) MUST be omitted from gt_responses. Any model output at a non-GT timestamp counts as a false positive.
  - Every trigger_time must be grounded in an event you actually observe in this specific video — do not fabricate timestamps.

[TASK]
Produce exactly 1 MQ sample(s). Each sample is a SET of 2-4 INTERRELATED questions — chosen so the cell's C x D combination is genuinely illustrated — plus the complete list of gt_responses attributed to those questions. Use stable question_ids like 'q_001', 'q_002', ... Each gt_responses entry MUST reference one of the sample's question_ids via its `question_id` field. Use each later question's `depends_on` field to point to the earlier qids it references (per the Axis C rule).

Each sample must:
  (a) match this cell's trigger pattern AND response complexity,
  (b) be grounded in events / objects / states actually present in this specific video — no generic templates,
  (c) yield MULTIPLE gt_responses across the video's duration (at least 2 entries; prefer 3-8),
  (d) demonstrate this cell's combination distinctively.

MQ_C1_D2 Independent × Concurrent / Overlapping ✓
C1 Independent — Multiple questions coexist but are semantically unrelated; the model must track them in parallel without interference.
D2 Concurrent / Overlapping — Multiple questions are active simultaneously; the model must track all of them in parallel.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic removing the wheels from a car. The sample features two independent, concurrent questions. The first question asks for a notification each time the mechanic finishes removing all lug nuts from a wheel, while the second asks for a notification each time he places those lug nuts in a magnetic tray. These two unrelated tasks must be tracked in parallel throughout the video, demonstrating the model's ability to handle concurrent, independent queries.

    q_00200:05.000:05.0null—Notify me every time the person places a set of lug nuts into the magnetic tray.

You are designing proactive streaming-video benchmark samples — questions AND their ground-truth response timestamps — for a single video.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an MQ (Multiple Questions, multiple answers) task, the model receives multiple questions at different points in time. Each question may require multiple responses (as in SQ), but the questions are interrelated — through logical dependencies, temporal relationships, or both. The model must concurrently track all active questions, manage their lifecycles, and respond to the appropriate question at the appropriate moment.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: MQ_C1_D2

Axis 1 — C1 Independent
Definition: Multiple questions coexist but are semantically unrelated; the model must track them in parallel without interference.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: "Remind me when I pick up the knife" + "Count how many times I add seasoning"
  - Manipulation: "Alert me on each grasp" + "Count completed assembly steps"
  - Navigation: "Alert me when I see a door" + "Count my turns"

Axis 2 — D2 Concurrent / Overlapping
Definition: Multiple questions are active simultaneously; the model must track all of them in parallel.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: t=0 "Alert me on every knife pickup" + t=30 "Also count my seasoning additions"
  - Manipulation: t=0 "Alert me on every grasp" + t=20 "Also count completed steps"
  - Navigation: t=0 "Alert me on every door" + t=30 "Also count my turns"

Reference combined example: see the cross-product of the two axes above — your task is to instantiate that combination in this video.

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis C (Logical Relationship between questions) rule:
  Independent — questions are semantically unrelated. GT for each question is generated independently and merged. `depends_on` stays empty for every question.

Axis D (Temporal Relationship between questions) rule:
  Concurrent / overlapping — multiple questions are active simultaneously. Each question's GT is kept within [activation_time, deactivation_time], possibly overlapping with other active questions. Use deactivation_time=null for questions that stay active until the video ends.

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.
  - Distractor events (events that LOOK like triggers but should NOT produce a response — e.g. red curtains for a 'red door' question, or doors after a cancel-doors command) MUST be omitted from gt_responses. Any model output at a non-GT timestamp counts as a false positive.
  - Every trigger_time must be grounded in an event you actually observe in this specific video — do not fabricate timestamps.

[TASK]
Produce exactly 1 MQ sample(s). Each sample is a SET of 2-4 INTERRELATED questions — chosen so the cell's C x D combination is genuinely illustrated — plus the complete list of gt_responses attributed to those questions. Use stable question_ids like 'q_001', 'q_002', ... Each gt_responses entry MUST reference one of the sample's question_ids via its `question_id` field. Use each later question's `depends_on` field to point to the earlier qids it references (per the Axis C rule).

Each sample must:
  (a) match this cell's trigger pattern AND response complexity,
  (b) be grounded in events / objects / states actually present in this specific video — no generic templates,
  (c) yield MULTIPLE gt_responses across the video's duration (at least 2 entries; prefer 3-8),
  (d) demonstrate this cell's combination distinctively.

MQ_C1_D3 Independent × Lifecycle Control ✓
C1 Independent — Multiple questions coexist but are semantically unrelated; the model must track them in parallel without interference.
D3 Lifecycle Control — The relationship between questions is one of explicit start, pause, resume, or cancel.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic removing the tires from a car. The sample features two independent tracking questions issued at the start: one for when a tire is removed, and another for when a tool is picked up. Later in the video, two separate 'cancel' commands are issued, one for each of the initial questions. This demonstrates the model's ability to handle independent, parallel queries (C1) and manage their lifecycles with explicit commands (D3), correctly ignoring events that occur after a task has been canceled.

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    qidissuedactivedeactive depends_onquestion
    q_00100:00.000:00.002:10.0—Notify me each time the mechanic removes a tire from the car.
    q_00200:05.000:05.001:55.0—Also, tell me whenever he picks up a tool.
    q_00301:55.001:55.001:55.0—Okay, stop telling me about the tools.
    q_00402:10.002:10.002:10.0—Cancel the tire removal alerts.
    qidt (mm:ss)response_window response_text
    q_00200:13.8[00:13.8, 00:14.8]The mechanic has picked up a tool.
    q_00200:19.9[00:19.9, 00:20.9]The mechanic has picked up a tool.
    q_00200:28.2[00:28.2, 00:29.2]The mechanic has picked up a tool.
    q_00101:59.8[01:59.8, 02:00.8]The mechanic has removed a tire.

You are designing proactive streaming-video benchmark samples — questions AND their ground-truth response timestamps — for a single video.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an MQ (Multiple Questions, multiple answers) task, the model receives multiple questions at different points in time. Each question may require multiple responses (as in SQ), but the questions are interrelated — through logical dependencies, temporal relationships, or both. The model must concurrently track all active questions, manage their lifecycles, and respond to the appropriate question at the appropriate moment.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: MQ_C1_D3

Axis 1 — C1 Independent
Definition: Multiple questions coexist but are semantically unrelated; the model must track them in parallel without interference.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: "Remind me when I pick up the knife" + "Count how many times I add seasoning"
  - Manipulation: "Alert me on each grasp" + "Count completed assembly steps"
  - Navigation: "Alert me when I see a door" + "Count my turns"

Axis 2 — D3 Lifecycle Control
Definition: The relationship between questions is one of explicit start, pause, resume, or cancel.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: t=0 "Alert me when the water boils" → t=120 "Stop alerting about the water" → t=180 "Resume the water alerts"
  - Manipulation: t=0 "Track my grasps" → t=60 "Pause that" → t=90 "Resume"
  - Navigation: t=0 "Count my turns" → t=120 "Cancel that"

Reference combined example: see the cross-product of the two axes above — your task is to instantiate that combination in this video.

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis C (Logical Relationship between questions) rule:
  Independent — questions are semantically unrelated. GT for each question is generated independently and merged. `depends_on` stays empty for every question.

Axis D (Temporal Relationship between questions) rule:
  Lifecycle control — some questions are explicit pause / resume / cancel commands that target a prior question. The command questions themselves do NOT emit any gt_responses; they only modify the active intervals of the targeted prior question, which must REMOVE its GT entries that fall in the now-inactive interval.

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.
  - Distractor events (events that LOOK like triggers but should NOT produce a response — e.g. red curtains for a 'red door' question, or doors after a cancel-doors command) MUST be omitted from gt_responses. Any model output at a non-GT timestamp counts as a false positive.
  - Every trigger_time must be grounded in an event you actually observe in this specific video — do not fabricate timestamps.

[TASK]
Produce exactly 1 MQ sample(s). Each sample is a SET of 2-4 INTERRELATED questions — chosen so the cell's C x D combination is genuinely illustrated — plus the complete list of gt_responses attributed to those questions. Use stable question_ids like 'q_001', 'q_002', ... Each gt_responses entry MUST reference one of the sample's question_ids via its `question_id` field. Use each later question's `depends_on` field to point to the earlier qids it references (per the Axis C rule).

Each sample must:
  (a) match this cell's trigger pattern AND response complexity,
  (b) be grounded in events / objects / states actually present in this specific video — no generic templates,
  (c) yield MULTIPLE gt_responses across the video's duration (at least 2 entries; prefer 3-8),
  (d) demonstrate this cell's combination distinctively.

MQ_C1_D4 Independent × Triggered Activation ✓
C1 Independent — Multiple questions coexist but are semantically unrelated; the model must track them in parallel without interference.
D4 Triggered Activation — A new question is issued but does not take effect immediately — it becomes active only after a specified event occurs.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic removing all four wheels from a car. The first question asks to identify each time a wheel is removed, which is active from the beginning. The second question is activated only after the mechanic picks up the impact wrench, and then asks to identify every time he places lug nuts down. This sample fits the cell because the two questions are independent (C1), and the second question has a triggered activation based on a specific event in the video (D4).

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    qidissuedactivedeactive depends_onquestion
    q_00100:01.000:01.0null—Alert me every time a wheel is removed from the vehicle.
    q_00200:10.000:28.5null—After the mechanic grabs the impact wrench, let me know each time he puts the lug nuts down.
    qidt (mm:ss)response_window response_text
    q_00200:55.3[00:55.3, 00:56.3]The mechanic has put down the lug nuts.
    q_00201:15.4[01:15.4, 01:16.4]The mechanic has put down the lug nuts.
    q_00201:35.7[01:35.7, 01:36.7]The mechanic has put down the lug nuts.
    q_00201:55.5[01:55.5, 01:56.5]The mechanic has put down the lug nuts.
    q_00102:00.0[02:00.0, 02:01.0]A wheel has been removed from the vehicle.
    q_00102:17.5[02:17.5, 02:18.5]A wheel has been removed from the vehicle.

You are designing proactive streaming-video benchmark samples — questions AND their ground-truth response timestamps — for a single video.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an MQ (Multiple Questions, multiple answers) task, the model receives multiple questions at different points in time. Each question may require multiple responses (as in SQ), but the questions are interrelated — through logical dependencies, temporal relationships, or both. The model must concurrently track all active questions, manage their lifecycles, and respond to the appropriate question at the appropriate moment.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: MQ_C1_D4

Axis 1 — C1 Independent
Definition: Multiple questions coexist but are semantically unrelated; the model must track them in parallel without interference.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: "Remind me when I pick up the knife" + "Count how many times I add seasoning"
  - Manipulation: "Alert me on each grasp" + "Count completed assembly steps"
  - Navigation: "Alert me when I see a door" + "Count my turns"

Axis 2 — D4 Triggered Activation
Definition: A new question is issued but does not take effect immediately — it becomes active only after a specified event occurs.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: "Once I finish cutting, start alerting me on every seasoning"
  - Manipulation: "Once I pick up the hammer, start counting my hits"
  - Navigation: "Once I enter the kitchen, start alerting me on every appliance"

Reference combined example: see the cross-product of the two axes above — your task is to instantiate that combination in this video.

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis C (Logical Relationship between questions) rule:
  Independent — questions are semantically unrelated. GT for each question is generated independently and merged. `depends_on` stays empty for every question.

Axis D (Temporal Relationship between questions) rule:
  Triggered activation — the question's activation_time is determined by a precondition event in the video and is strictly LATER than its issue_time. Matching events BEFORE activation_time are EXCLUDED from gt_responses.

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.
  - Distractor events (events that LOOK like triggers but should NOT produce a response — e.g. red curtains for a 'red door' question, or doors after a cancel-doors command) MUST be omitted from gt_responses. Any model output at a non-GT timestamp counts as a false positive.
  - Every trigger_time must be grounded in an event you actually observe in this specific video — do not fabricate timestamps.

[TASK]
Produce exactly 1 MQ sample(s). Each sample is a SET of 2-4 INTERRELATED questions — chosen so the cell's C x D combination is genuinely illustrated — plus the complete list of gt_responses attributed to those questions. Use stable question_ids like 'q_001', 'q_002', ... Each gt_responses entry MUST reference one of the sample's question_ids via its `question_id` field. Use each later question's `depends_on` field to point to the earlier qids it references (per the Axis C rule).

Each sample must:
  (a) match this cell's trigger pattern AND response complexity,
  (b) be grounded in events / objects / states actually present in this specific video — no generic templates,
  (c) yield MULTIPLE gt_responses across the video's duration (at least 2 entries; prefer 3-8),
  (d) demonstrate this cell's combination distinctively.

MQ_C2_D1 Refining × Sequential / Non-overlapping ✓
C2 Refining — A later question modifies the scope of an earlier one — narrowing, broadening, or otherwise reshaping its semantics.
D1 Sequential / Non-overlapping — A new question is issued only after the previous one has completed or been explicitly closed. Active scopes do not overlap.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic removing the lug nuts from all four wheels of a car. The sample illustrates a C2_D1 scenario by first asking to be notified whenever the mechanic finishes with a wheel, and then issuing a sequential, refining question to only report on the rear wheels. This creates a non-overlapping temporal structure where the second question narrows the scope of the first, requiring the model to correctly apply the new constraint after it is issued.

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    qidissuedactivedeactive depends_onquestion
    q_00100:40.000:40.001:55.0—Tell me each time the mechanic has removed all the lug nuts from a wheel.
    q_00201:55.001:55.0nullq_001Actually, from this point on, only tell me when he is done with a rear wheel.
    qidt (mm:ss)response_window response_text
    q_00100:52.5[00:52.5, 00:53.5]The mechanic has finished removing the lug nuts from the front-right wheel.
    q_00101:53.0[01:53.0, 01:54.0]The mechanic has finished removing the lug nuts from the front-left wheel.
    q_00202:13.5[02:13.5, 02:14.5]The mechanic has finished removing the lug nuts from the rear-left wheel.
    q_00202:31.0[02:31.0, 02:32.0]The mechanic has finished removing the lug nuts from the rear-right wheel.

You are designing proactive streaming-video benchmark samples — questions AND their ground-truth response timestamps — for a single video.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an MQ (Multiple Questions, multiple answers) task, the model receives multiple questions at different points in time. Each question may require multiple responses (as in SQ), but the questions are interrelated — through logical dependencies, temporal relationships, or both. The model must concurrently track all active questions, manage their lifecycles, and respond to the appropriate question at the appropriate moment.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: MQ_C2_D1

Axis 1 — C2 Refining
Definition: A later question modifies the scope of an earlier one — narrowing, broadening, or otherwise reshaping its semantics.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: "Alert me on every seasoning" → "Actually, only alert me when I add salt"
  - Manipulation: "Alert me on every tool grasp" → "Only metallic tools"
  - Navigation: "Alert me on every door" → "Only open doors"

Axis 2 — D1 Sequential / Non-overlapping
Definition: A new question is issued only after the previous one has completed or been explicitly closed. Active scopes do not overlap.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: t=0 "Alert me when I finish cutting" → (after cutting) → t=60 "Now alert me when I add water"
  - Manipulation: t=0 "Alert me when I finish tightening" → (done) → t=45 "Next, alert me when I pick up the next tool"
  - Navigation: t=0 "Tell me when I reach the kitchen" → (arrived) → t=30 "Now tell me when I reach the bedroom"

Reference combined example: see the cross-product of the two axes above — your task is to instantiate that combination in this video.

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis C (Logical Relationship between questions) rule:
  Refining — a later question NARROWS or BROADENS the scope of an earlier question. After the refining question's issue_time, remove the earlier question's GT entries that no longer match the refined scope. List the earlier qid in the refining question's `depends_on`.

Axis D (Temporal Relationship between questions) rule:
  Sequential / non-overlapping — questions do not overlap in time. Each question's GT is restricted to events within [issue_time, next_question_issue_time). Set deactivation_time of each question to the next question's issue_time (or null for the last question).

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.
  - Distractor events (events that LOOK like triggers but should NOT produce a response — e.g. red curtains for a 'red door' question, or doors after a cancel-doors command) MUST be omitted from gt_responses. Any model output at a non-GT timestamp counts as a false positive.
  - Every trigger_time must be grounded in an event you actually observe in this specific video — do not fabricate timestamps.

[TASK]
Produce exactly 1 MQ sample(s). Each sample is a SET of 2-4 INTERRELATED questions — chosen so the cell's C x D combination is genuinely illustrated — plus the complete list of gt_responses attributed to those questions. Use stable question_ids like 'q_001', 'q_002', ... Each gt_responses entry MUST reference one of the sample's question_ids via its `question_id` field. Use each later question's `depends_on` field to point to the earlier qids it references (per the Axis C rule).

Each sample must:
  (a) match this cell's trigger pattern AND response complexity,
  (b) be grounded in events / objects / states actually present in this specific video — no generic templates,
  (c) yield MULTIPLE gt_responses across the video's duration (at least 2 entries; prefer 3-8),
  (d) demonstrate this cell's combination distinctively.

MQ_C2_D2 Refining × Concurrent / Overlapping ✓
C2 Refining — A later question modifies the scope of an earlier one — narrowing, broadening, or otherwise reshaping its semantics.
D2 Concurrent / Overlapping — Multiple questions are active simultaneously; the model must track all of them in parallel.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic removing all four wheels from a car. The sample demonstrates a refining, concurrent question set. The first question asks for all tool usage, which is then refined by a second question to only include *power* tools. Concurrently, a third question tracks when the lug nuts for each wheel are fully removed, forcing the model to monitor multiple, overlapping event types. This combination tests the model's ability to modify an ongoing query while simultaneously tracking an independent one.

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    qidissuedactivedeactive depends_onquestion
    q_00100:10.000:10.000:30.0—Alert me every time the mechanic begins using a tool.
    q_00200:30.000:30.0nullq_001Actually, from now on only alert me when he uses a power tool.
    q_00300:35.000:35.0null—Concurrently, let me know each time a full set of lug nuts has been removed from a wheel.
    qidt (mm:ss)response_window response_text
    q_00100:16.0[00:16.0, 00:17.0]The mechanic is using a socket.
    q_00100:19.5[00:19.5, 00:20.5]The mechanic is using an air hose.
    q_00100:28.0[00:28.0, 00:29.0]The mechanic is using a pneumatic impact wrench.
    q_00200:42.0[00:42.0, 00:43.0]The mechanic is using a power tool (impact wrench).
    q_00300:52.0[00:52.0, 00:53.0]The lug nuts have been removed from the first wheel.
    q_00201:42.0[01:42.0, 01:43.0]The mechanic is using a power tool (impact wrench).
    q_00301:53.0[01:53.0, 01:54.0]The lug nuts have been removed from the second wheel.
    q_00202:04.0[02:04.0, 02:05.0]The mechanic is using a power tool (impact wrench).
    q_00302:13.0[02:13.0, 02:14.0]The lug nuts have been removed from the third wheel.
    q_00202:20.0[02:20.0, 02:21.0]The mechanic is using a power tool (impact wrench).
    q_00302:31.0[02:31.0, 02:32.0]The lug nuts have been removed from the fourth wheel.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an MQ (Multiple Questions, multiple answers) task, the model receives multiple questions at different points in time. Each question may require multiple responses (as in SQ), but the questions are interrelated — through logical dependencies, temporal relationships, or both. The model must concurrently track all active questions, manage their lifecycles, and respond to the appropriate question at the appropriate moment.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: MQ_C2_D2

Axis 1 — C2 Refining
Definition: A later question modifies the scope of an earlier one — narrowing, broadening, or otherwise reshaping its semantics.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: "Alert me on every seasoning" → "Actually, only alert me when I add salt"
  - Manipulation: "Alert me on every tool grasp" → "Only metallic tools"
  - Navigation: "Alert me on every door" → "Only open doors"

Axis 2 — D2 Concurrent / Overlapping
Definition: Multiple questions are active simultaneously; the model must track all of them in parallel.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: t=0 "Alert me on every knife pickup" + t=30 "Also count my seasoning additions"
  - Manipulation: t=0 "Alert me on every grasp" + t=20 "Also count completed steps"
  - Navigation: t=0 "Alert me on every door" + t=30 "Also count my turns"

Reference combined example: see the cross-product of the two axes above — your task is to instantiate that combination in this video.

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis C (Logical Relationship between questions) rule:
  Refining — a later question NARROWS or BROADENS the scope of an earlier question. After the refining question's issue_time, remove the earlier question's GT entries that no longer match the refined scope. List the earlier qid in the refining question's `depends_on`.

Axis D (Temporal Relationship between questions) rule:
  Concurrent / overlapping — multiple questions are active simultaneously. Each question's GT is kept within [activation_time, deactivation_time], possibly overlapping with other active questions. Use deactivation_time=null for questions that stay active until the video ends.

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.
  - Distractor events (events that LOOK like triggers but should NOT produce a response — e.g. red curtains for a 'red door' question, or doors after a cancel-doors command) MUST be omitted from gt_responses. Any model output at a non-GT timestamp counts as a false positive.
  - Every trigger_time must be grounded in an event you actually observe in this specific video — do not fabricate timestamps.

[TASK]
Produce exactly 1 MQ sample(s). Each sample is a SET of 2-4 INTERRELATED questions — chosen so the cell's C x D combination is genuinely illustrated — plus the complete list of gt_responses attributed to those questions. Use stable question_ids like 'q_001', 'q_002', ... Each gt_responses entry MUST reference one of the sample's question_ids via its `question_id` field. Use each later question's `depends_on` field to point to the earlier qids it references (per the Axis C rule).

Each sample must:
  (a) match this cell's trigger pattern AND response complexity,
  (b) be grounded in events / objects / states actually present in this specific video — no generic templates,
  (c) yield MULTIPLE gt_responses across the video's duration (at least 2 entries; prefer 3-8),
  (d) demonstrate this cell's combination distinctively.

MQ_C2_D3 Refining × Lifecycle Control ✓
C2 Refining — A later question modifies the scope of an earlier one — narrowing, broadening, or otherwise reshaping its semantics.
D3 Lifecycle Control — The relationship between questions is one of explicit start, pause, resume, or cancel.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic removing all four wheels from a car. The sample demonstrates a combination of refining scope (C2) and lifecycle control (D3). An initial broad question about tool usage is narrowed to only focus on the impact wrench. Subsequently, commands are issued to pause and later resume the alerts for this refined task, showcasing explicit control over the question's activity.

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    qidissuedactivedeactive depends_onquestion
    q_00100:05.000:05.0null—Alert me every time a tool is used on the car.
    q_00200:30.000:30.0nullq_001Actually, only alert me when the impact wrench is used.
    q_00300:55.000:55.0nullq_001Pause the impact wrench alerts for now.
    q_00401:35.001:35.0nullq_001Resume the impact wrench alerts.
    qidt (mm:ss)response_window response_text
    q_00100:16.0[00:16.0, 00:17.0]A tool is being used on the car.
    q_00100:42.0[00:42.0, 00:43.0]The impact wrench is being used on the car.
    q_00100:44.0[00:44.0, 00:45.0]The impact wrench is being used on the car.
    q_00100:46.0[00:46.0, 00:47.0]The impact wrench is being used on the car.
    q_00100:48.0[00:48.0, 00:49.0]The impact wrench is being used on the car.
    q_00100:50.0[00:50.0, 00:51.0]The impact wrench is being used on the car.
    q_00101:40.0[01:40.0, 01:41.0]The impact wrench is being used on the car.
    q_00101:42.0[01:42.0, 01:43.0]The impact wrench is being used on the car.
    q_00101:44.0[01:44.0, 01:45.0]The impact wrench is being used on the car.
    q_00101:46.0[01:46.0, 01:47.0]The impact wrench is being used on the car.
    q_00101:48.0[01:48.0, 01:49.0]The impact wrench is being used on the car.

You are designing proactive streaming-video benchmark samples — questions AND their ground-truth response timestamps — for a single video.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an MQ (Multiple Questions, multiple answers) task, the model receives multiple questions at different points in time. Each question may require multiple responses (as in SQ), but the questions are interrelated — through logical dependencies, temporal relationships, or both. The model must concurrently track all active questions, manage their lifecycles, and respond to the appropriate question at the appropriate moment.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: MQ_C2_D3

Axis 1 — C2 Refining
Definition: A later question modifies the scope of an earlier one — narrowing, broadening, or otherwise reshaping its semantics.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: "Alert me on every seasoning" → "Actually, only alert me when I add salt"
  - Manipulation: "Alert me on every tool grasp" → "Only metallic tools"
  - Navigation: "Alert me on every door" → "Only open doors"

Axis 2 — D3 Lifecycle Control
Definition: The relationship between questions is one of explicit start, pause, resume, or cancel.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: t=0 "Alert me when the water boils" → t=120 "Stop alerting about the water" → t=180 "Resume the water alerts"
  - Manipulation: t=0 "Track my grasps" → t=60 "Pause that" → t=90 "Resume"
  - Navigation: t=0 "Count my turns" → t=120 "Cancel that"

Reference combined example: see the cross-product of the two axes above — your task is to instantiate that combination in this video.

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis C (Logical Relationship between questions) rule:
  Refining — a later question NARROWS or BROADENS the scope of an earlier question. After the refining question's issue_time, remove the earlier question's GT entries that no longer match the refined scope. List the earlier qid in the refining question's `depends_on`.

Axis D (Temporal Relationship between questions) rule:
  Lifecycle control — some questions are explicit pause / resume / cancel commands that target a prior question. The command questions themselves do NOT emit any gt_responses; they only modify the active intervals of the targeted prior question, which must REMOVE its GT entries that fall in the now-inactive interval.

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.
  - Distractor events (events that LOOK like triggers but should NOT produce a response — e.g. red curtains for a 'red door' question, or doors after a cancel-doors command) MUST be omitted from gt_responses. Any model output at a non-GT timestamp counts as a false positive.
  - Every trigger_time must be grounded in an event you actually observe in this specific video — do not fabricate timestamps.

[TASK]
Produce exactly 1 MQ sample(s). Each sample is a SET of 2-4 INTERRELATED questions — chosen so the cell's C x D combination is genuinely illustrated — plus the complete list of gt_responses attributed to those questions. Use stable question_ids like 'q_001', 'q_002', ... Each gt_responses entry MUST reference one of the sample's question_ids via its `question_id` field. Use each later question's `depends_on` field to point to the earlier qids it references (per the Axis C rule).

Each sample must:
  (a) match this cell's trigger pattern AND response complexity,
  (b) be grounded in events / objects / states actually present in this specific video — no generic templates,
  (c) yield MULTIPLE gt_responses across the video's duration (at least 2 entries; prefer 3-8),
  (d) demonstrate this cell's combination distinctively.

MQ_C2_D4 Refining × Triggered Activation ✓
C2 Refining — A later question modifies the scope of an earlier one — narrowing, broadening, or otherwise reshaping its semantics.
D4 Triggered Activation — A new question is issued but does not take effect immediately — it becomes active only after a specified event occurs.
Rationale

This sample demonstrates a refining question with a triggered activation. The initial question asks to be notified when the mechanic finishes removing all lug nuts from any wheel. A second, refining question is issued, which narrows the scope to only wheels on the driver's side, but this refinement only becomes active after the mechanic removes the first wheel from the car. This tests the model's ability to handle a deferred change in scope based on a video event, correctly identifying the target event before the trigger, ignoring a distractor event after the trigger, and then correctly identifying subsequent target events that match the refined criteria.

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    qidissuedactivedeactive depends_onquestion
    q_00100:10.000:10.0null—Notify me when the mechanic finishes removing all the lug nuts from a wheel.
    q_00200:15.000:52.5nullq_001After the mechanic removes the first wheel from the car, change the previous alert to only trigger for wheels on the driver's side.
    qidt (mm:ss)response_window response_text
    q_00100:51.5[00:51.5, 00:52.5]The mechanic has finished removing the lug nuts from the front passenger side wheel.
    q_00101:32.5[01:32.5, 01:33.5]The mechanic has finished removing the lug nuts from the rear driver's side wheel.
    q_00101:50.5[01:50.5, 01:51.5]The mechanic has finished removing the lug nuts from the front driver's side wheel.
Show full per-cell prompt sent to Gemini 
You are designing proactive streaming-video benchmark samples — questions AND their ground-truth response timestamps — for a single video.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an MQ (Multiple Questions, multiple answers) task, the model receives multiple questions at different points in time. Each question may require multiple responses (as in SQ), but the questions are interrelated — through logical dependencies, temporal relationships, or both. The model must concurrently track all active questions, manage their lifecycles, and respond to the appropriate question at the appropriate moment.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: MQ_C2_D4

Axis 1 — C2 Refining
Definition: A later question modifies the scope of an earlier one — narrowing, broadening, or otherwise reshaping its semantics.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: "Alert me on every seasoning" → "Actually, only alert me when I add salt"
  - Manipulation: "Alert me on every tool grasp" → "Only metallic tools"
  - Navigation: "Alert me on every door" → "Only open doors"

Axis 2 — D4 Triggered Activation
Definition: A new question is issued but does not take effect immediately — it becomes active only after a specified event occurs.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: "Once I finish cutting, start alerting me on every seasoning"
  - Manipulation: "Once I pick up the hammer, start counting my hits"
  - Navigation: "Once I enter the kitchen, start alerting me on every appliance"

Reference combined example: see the cross-product of the two axes above — your task is to instantiate that combination in this video.

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis C (Logical Relationship between questions) rule:
  Refining — a later question NARROWS or BROADENS the scope of an earlier question. After the refining question's issue_time, remove the earlier question's GT entries that no longer match the refined scope. List the earlier qid in the refining question's `depends_on`.

Axis D (Temporal Relationship between questions) rule:
  Triggered activation — the question's activation_time is determined by a precondition event in the video and is strictly LATER than its issue_time. Matching events BEFORE activation_time are EXCLUDED from gt_responses.

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.
  - Distractor events (events that LOOK like triggers but should NOT produce a response — e.g. red curtains for a 'red door' question, or doors after a cancel-doors command) MUST be omitted from gt_responses. Any model output at a non-GT timestamp counts as a false positive.
  - Every trigger_time must be grounded in an event you actually observe in this specific video — do not fabricate timestamps.

[TASK]
Produce exactly 1 MQ sample(s). Each sample is a SET of 2-4 INTERRELATED questions — chosen so the cell's C x D combination is genuinely illustrated — plus the complete list of gt_responses attributed to those questions. Use stable question_ids like 'q_001', 'q_002', ... Each gt_responses entry MUST reference one of the sample's question_ids via its `question_id` field. Use each later question's `depends_on` field to point to the earlier qids it references (per the Axis C rule).

Each sample must:
  (a) match this cell's trigger pattern AND response complexity,
  (b) be grounded in events / objects / states actually present in this specific video — no generic templates,
  (c) yield MULTIPLE gt_responses across the video's duration (at least 2 entries; prefer 3-8),
  (d) demonstrate this cell's combination distinctively.

MQ_C3_D1 Compositional × Sequential / Non-overlapping ✓
C3 Compositional — A new question references the state or result of a prior question, requiring the model to maintain and reuse memory across instructions.
D1 Sequential / Non-overlapping — A new question is issued only after the previous one has completed or been explicitly closed. Active scopes do not overlap.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic removing lug nuts from all four wheels of a car. The first question asks the model to identify when the first set of lug nuts is removed. The second question, issued sequentially after the first is answered, builds on this by asking for notifications for all subsequent wheels. This demonstrates a compositional relationship (C3) where the second question depends on the context of the first, and a sequential, non-overlapping temporal pattern (D1).

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    qidissuedactivedeactive depends_onquestion
    q_00100:10.000:10.000:53.0—Keep track of each time the mechanic removes the full set of lug nuts from a wheel. Let me know when this happens for the first time.
    q_00200:53.000:53.0nullq_001Now, for each subsequent wheel he works on, tell me when he finishes removing the lug nuts.
    qidt (mm:ss)response_window response_text
    q_00100:52.0[00:52.0, 00:53.0]The mechanic has finished removing the lug nuts from the first wheel.
    q_00201:13.0[01:13.0, 01:14.0]He has finished removing the lug nuts from a subsequent wheel.
    q_00201:34.0[01:34.0, 01:35.0]He has finished removing the lug nuts from a subsequent wheel.
    q_00201:51.0[01:51.0, 01:52.0]He has finished removing the lug nuts from a subsequent wheel.

You are designing proactive streaming-video benchmark samples — questions AND their ground-truth response timestamps — for a single video.

[TASK FAMILY DEFINITION]
In an MQ (Multiple Questions, multiple answers) task, the model receives multiple questions at different points in time. Each question may require multiple responses (as in SQ), but the questions are interrelated — through logical dependencies, temporal relationships, or both. The model must concurrently track all active questions, manage their lifecycles, and respond to the appropriate question at the appropriate moment.

[CELL DEFINITION]
Cell: MQ_C3_D1

Axis 1 — C3 Compositional
Definition: A new question references the state or result of a prior question, requiring the model to maintain and reuse memory across instructions.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: "Remember every tool I use" + "Tell me when I use the first one again"
  - Manipulation: "Track the order of tools I pick up" + "Tell me when I use them in the same order again"
  - Navigation: "Remember the rooms I visit" + "Tell me when I return to the first room"

Axis 2 — D1 Sequential / Non-overlapping
Definition: A new question is issued only after the previous one has completed or been explicitly closed. Active scopes do not overlap.
Examples across domains:
  - Kitchen: t=0 "Alert me when I finish cutting" → (after cutting) → t=60 "Now alert me when I add water"
  - Manipulation: t=0 "Alert me when I finish tightening" → (done) → t=45 "Next, alert me when I pick up the next tool"
  - Navigation: t=0 "Tell me when I reach the kitchen" → (arrived) → t=30 "Now tell me when I reach the bedroom"

Reference combined example: see the cross-product of the two axes above — your task is to instantiate that combination in this video.

[GROUND-TRUTH GENERATION RULES]
Watch the video carefully. Note its total duration in seconds — every trigger_time you emit must lie between 0.0 and that duration.

Axis C (Logical Relationship between questions) rule:
  Compositional — a later question references the state or result of an earlier question (e.g. "the first object I used"). Its response_text is computed from the accumulated history of the earlier question(s). List the earlier qid(s) in `depends_on`.

Axis D (Temporal Relationship between questions) rule:
  Sequential / non-overlapping — questions do not overlap in time. Each question's GT is restricted to events within [issue_time, next_question_issue_time). Set deactivation_time of each question to the next question's issue_time (or null for the last question).

Universal constraints (apply to every entry, all cells):
  - trigger_time is in SECONDS from the start of the video, as a float (e.g. 12.3, 47.8).
  - response_window MUST be uniformly [trigger_time, trigger_time + 1.0].
  - response_text is a single canonical answer string; evaluation uses an LLM judge for semantic equivalence.
  - Distractor events (events that LOOK like triggers but should NOT produce a response — e.g. red curtains for a 'red door' question, or doors after a cancel-doors command) MUST be omitted from gt_responses. Any model output at a non-GT timestamp counts as a false positive.
  - Every trigger_time must be grounded in an event you actually observe in this specific video — do not fabricate timestamps.

[TASK]
Produce exactly 1 MQ sample(s). Each sample is a SET of 2-4 INTERRELATED questions — chosen so the cell's C x D combination is genuinely illustrated — plus the complete list of gt_responses attributed to those questions. Use stable question_ids like 'q_001', 'q_002', ... Each gt_responses entry MUST reference one of the sample's question_ids via its `question_id` field. Use each later question's `depends_on` field to point to the earlier qids it references (per the Axis C rule).

Each sample must:
  (a) match this cell's trigger pattern AND response complexity,
  (b) be grounded in events / objects / states actually present in this specific video — no generic templates,
  (c) yield MULTIPLE gt_responses across the video's duration (at least 2 entries; prefer 3-8),
  (d) demonstrate this cell's combination distinctively.

MQ_C3_D2 Compositional × Concurrent / Overlapping ✓
C3 Compositional — A new question references the state or result of a prior question, requiring the model to maintain and reuse memory across instructions.
D2 Concurrent / Overlapping — Multiple questions are active simultaneously; the model must track all of them in parallel.
Rationale

This video shows a mechanic removing wheels from a car. The chosen questions demonstrate a compositional and concurrent task. One question asks to identify each new tool as it is used, while a second, concurrent question asks to report every time the *first* identified tool is used again. A third concurrent question tracks a separate, high-level action (wheel removal), ensuring the model must manage multiple, overlapping tasks with different trigger conditions.

Samples (1)
  1. Sample 1
    qidissuedactivedeactive depends_onquestion
    q_00100:00.000:00.0null—Tell me what tool the mechanic is using when they first use it.
    q_00200:10.000:10.0nullq_001Tell me every time the first tool identified is used again.
    q_00300:05.000:05.0null—Notify me every time the mechanic removes a wheel.
    qidt (mm:ss)response_window response_text
    q_00100:16.5[00:16.5, 00:17.5]The mechanic is using a socket.
    q_00100:42.2[00:42.2, 00:43.2]The mechanic is using an impact wrench.
    q_00200:42.2[00:42.2, 00:43.2]The socket is being used again.
    q_00201:42.1[01:42.1, 01:43.1]The socket is being used again.
    q_00301:58.5[01:58.5, 01:59.5]The mechanic is removing a wheel.
    q_00202:04.1[02:04.1, 02:05.1]The socket is being used again.
    q_00302:15.5[02:15.5, 02:16.5]The mechanic is removing a wheel.
    q_00202:20.3[02:20.3, 02:21.3]The socket is being used again.
    q_00102:34.5[02:34.5, 02:35.5]The mechanic is using a flashlight.
